message:
fix(worktree): replace heredoc with printf for test compatibility

The update_digest_cache function used a heredoc that contained a closing
brace on its own line. This broke source_ru_function extraction in test
framework which uses sed '/^func()/,/^}/p' pattern.

Changes:
- Replace heredoc in update_digest_cache with printf for JSON generation
- Fix test assertions to match actual JSON field names (path vs worktree_path)
- Fix test calls to use correct argument count for record_worktree_mapping
- Add missing digest cache tests (bd-ai1z acceptance criteria)

Test results: 22 unit tests pass, 7 E2E tests pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-8op0","title":"Define ntm workflow pipeline for github-review","description":"Task: Define ntm Workflow Pipeline for github-review

Purpose
-------
Create the github-review.yaml workflow that orchestrates the multi-step
review process through ntm workflow engine.

Background: ntm Workflows
-------------------------
ntm supports YAML-defined workflows with:
- Sequential and parallel steps
- Agent (Claude) steps with prompts
- Shell steps for scripting
- Health checks and timeouts
- Question queuing with priorities
- Input/output variables

Workflow Location
-----------------
~/.config/ntm/workflows/github-review.yaml

Workflow Schema
---------------
schema_version: "2.0"
name: github-review
description: |
  Automated GitHub issue and PR review workflow (Plan Mode).
  Agent produces local patches + review-plan.json artifact.
  NO direct GitHub mutations.

inputs:
  worktree_path: (required) Path to isolated worktree
  repo_name: (required) GitHub repo identifier
  repo_digest_path: (optional) Cached digest path
  work_items: (required) JSON array of items to review

settings:
  timeout: "45m"
  on_error: "fail"
  notify_on_error: true

Steps
-----

1. verify_prerequisites (shell)
   - Check gh auth status
   - Verify worktree exists
   - Create .ru directory

2. understand_codebase (agent)
   - Read AGENTS.md and README.md
   - Load or create repo digest
   - Update digest if needed
   - Timeout: 10m
   - Health check every 30s

3. review_issues_prs (agent)
   - Main review work
   - Use gh for READ only
   - Create commits for fixes
   - Produce review-plan.json
   - Queue questions with priority
   - Timeout: 30m
   - Wait for user interaction

4. finalize_artifacts (shell)
   - Verify review-plan.json exists
   - Validate JSON structure
   - Log completion

Outputs
-------
  plan_path: Path to review-plan.json
  digest_path: Path to repo-digest.md
  items_reviewed: Count of items reviewed

Question Handling
-----------------
on_question:
  action: queue
  priority: from question urgency
  metadata:
    repo: repo_name
    worktree: worktree_path

This routes questions to ru TUI for aggregation.

Error Handling
--------------
- on_failure: abort (for prerequisites)
- on_failure: warn (for finalize)
- Health checks detect stalls
- Timeout prevents runaway sessions

Testing
-------
- Dry run workflow validation
- Step execution order
- Question queuing works
- Timeout handling
- Error propagation

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Workflow file validates
- [ ] All steps execute in order
- [ ] Questions queued correctly
- [ ] Timeouts enforced

-{"id":"bd-8q3s","title":"Phase 3: Session Driver & Spawning","description":"# Phase 3: Session Driver & Spawning

## Objective
Implement a unified session driver interface that works with or without ntm, providing the same interface for both modes. The driver launches Claude Code sessions, sends prompts, and streams events.

## Architecture

### Unified Session Driver Interface
```bash
# Interface functions (implemented differently per driver)
session_driver_start()     # repo_ctx -> session_handle
session_driver_send()      # session, message
session_driver_stream()    # session -> normalized_events
session_driver_interrupt() # session
session_driver_stop()      # session
```

### Normalized Event Schema
Both drivers emit the same event schema:
```json
{
  "type": "init|generating|waiting|complete|error",
  "session_id": "...",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-08T12:00:00Z",
  "data": {
    "reason": "ask_user_question|agent_question|external_prompt",
    "question": {...},
    "status": "success|error"
  }
}
```

## Implementation

### 3.1 Driver Detection
```bash
detect_review_driver() {
    if command -v ntm &>/dev/null && ntm --version &>/dev/null; then
        echo "ntm"
    else
        echo "local"
    fi
}
```

### 3.2 Local Driver (tmux + stream-json)
```bash
local_driver_start() {
    local worktree_path="$1"
    local session_id="ru-review-$$-$(basename "$worktree_path")"

    # Create named pipe for output
    local pipe_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/pipes/${session_id}.pipe"
    mkfifo "$pipe_file"

    # Launch Claude Code with stream-json
    tmux new-session -d -s "$session_id" -c "$worktree_path" \
        "claude -p '...' --output-format stream-json 2>&1 | tee $pipe_file"

    echo "$session_id"
}

local_driver_stream() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local pipe_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/pipes/${session_id}.pipe"

    while IFS= read -r line; do
        normalize_claude_event "$line"
    done < "$pipe_file"
}
```

### 3.3 ntm Driver (Robot Mode API)
```bash
ntm_driver_start() {
    local worktree_path="$1"
    local session_name="ru-review-$$"

    ntm --robot-spawn "$session_name" \
        --cc=1 \
        --working-dir="$worktree_path"
}

ntm_driver_stream() {
    local session_id="$1"
    ntm --robot-stream="$session_id" --format=json
}

ntm_driver_send() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
    ntm --robot-send="$session_id" --msg="$message"
}
```

### 3.4 Prompt Chunking
tmux has ~4KB practical limit per SendKeys:
```bash
ntm_send_prompt_chunked() {
    local session="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local chunk_size=3500

    local offset=0 length=${#prompt}
    while [[ $offset -lt $length ]]; do
        local chunk="${prompt:$offset:$chunk_size}"
        ntm --robot-send="$session" --msg="$chunk"
        ((offset += chunk_size))
        sleep 0.1
    done
}
```

### 3.5 Review Prompt Generation
```bash
generate_review_prompt() {
    local repo_name="$1"
    local worktree_path="$2"
    local run_id="$3"
    local work_items_json="$4"

    cat <<EOF
We don't allow PRs or outside contributions...
[Full policy prompt from EXTENDING doc]

WORK ITEMS TO REVIEW:
$work_items_json

REQUIRED OUTPUT:
You MUST produce .ru/review-plan.json conforming to schema v1.
EOF
}
```

## Unit Tests (scripts/test_unit_session_driver.sh)

1. **test_driver_detection_with_ntm**: Verify ntm detected when available
2. **test_driver_detection_without_ntm**: Verify local fallback
3. **test_local_session_start**: Verify tmux session created
4. **test_local_pipe_creation**: Verify named pipe created
5. **test_event_normalization**: Verify Claude events normalized correctly
6. **test_prompt_chunking**: Verify large prompts chunked at 3500 chars
7. **test_prompt_generation**: Verify prompt includes policy and work items
8. **test_session_cleanup**: Verify cleanup removes pipes and sessions

## E2E Tests (scripts/test_e2e_session_driver.sh)

1. **test_local_driver_full_cycle**:
   - Start session
   - Send prompt
   - Receive events
   - Session completes
   - Cleanup

2. **test_parallel_sessions**:
   - Start 4 sessions simultaneously
   - All produce events
   - No cross-talk between sessions

3. **test_session_interrupt_recovery**:
   - Start session
   - Send interrupt
   - Verify graceful shutdown

## Logging Requirements
- LOG_DEBUG: "Detected driver: $driver"
- LOG_DEBUG: "Starting session $session_id in $worktree_path"
- LOG_INFO: "Session $session_id started"
- LOG_DEBUG: "Event received: $event_type"
- LOG_DEBUG: "Sending prompt (${#prompt} chars, ${chunks} chunks)"
- LOG_WARN: "Session $session_id stalled"
- LOG_ERROR: "Session $session_id failed: $error"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Driver detection works correctly
- [ ] Local driver uses tmux + stream-json
- [ ] ntm driver uses robot mode API
- [ ] Both drivers emit same event schema
- [ ] Prompt chunking handles large prompts
- [ ] Named pipes cleaned up on exit
- [ ] All 8 unit tests pass

 {"id":"bd-8zt6","title":"Implement stream-json event parsing","description":"# Task: Implement Stream-JSON Event Parsing

## Purpose
Parse Claude Code's stream-json NDJSON output to extract events for programmatic control: session init, tool use, questions, and completion.

## Background: Stream-JSON Format
Claude Code with --output-format stream-json emits one JSON object per line:

### Event Types

#### 1. System Init
```json
{
  "type": "system",
  "subtype": "init",
  "session_id": "abc123",
  "tools": ["Read", "Write", "Edit", "Bash", "Glob", "Grep", ...]
}
```

#### 2. Assistant Message (Text)
```json
{
  "type": "assistant",
  "message": {
    "content": [
      {"type": "text", "text": "I'll analyze the codebase..."}
    ]
  }
}
```

#### 3. Assistant Message (Tool Use)
```json
{
  "type": "assistant",
  "message": {
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "tool_use",
        "id": "toolu_123",
        "name": "Read",
        "input": {"file_path": "/path/to/file"}
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

#### 4. AskUserQuestion Tool (CRITICAL)
```json
{
  "type": "assistant",
  "message": {
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "tool_use",
        "id": "toolu_456",
        "name": "AskUserQuestion",
        "input": {
          "questions": [{
            "question": "Should I refactor the entire auth module?",
            "header": "Approach",
            "options": [
              {"label": "Quick fix", "description": "Fix only this bug (5 lines)"},
              {"label": "Full refactor", "description": "Modernize auth module (200+ lines)"},
              {"label": "Skip", "description": "Not a priority right now"}
            ],
            "multiSelect": false
          }]
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

#### 5. Tool Result
```json
{
  "type": "user",
  "message": {
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "tool_result",
        "tool_use_id": "toolu_123",
        "content": "file contents..."
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

#### 6. Session Result
```json
{
  "type": "result",
  "status": "success",
  "duration_ms": 45000,
  "session_id": "abc123"
}
```

## Implementation

### parse_stream_json_event()
```bash
parse_stream_json_event() {
    local line="$1"
    local -n _event_type=$2
    local -n _event_data=$3
    
    # Validate JSON
    if ! echo "$line" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
        _event_type="invalid"
        _event_data="$line"
        return 1
    fi
    
    _event_type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"')
    
    case "$_event_type" in
        system)
            local subtype
            subtype=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.subtype // ""')
            if [[ "$subtype" == "init" ]]; then
                _event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '{session_id, tools}')
            fi
            ;;
        assistant)
            _event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.message.content')
            ;;
        user)
            _event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.message.content')
            ;;
        result)
            _event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '{status, duration_ms, session_id}')
            ;;
        *)
            _event_data="$line"
            ;;
    esac
    
    return 0
}
```

### detect_ask_user_question()
```bash
detect_ask_user_question() {
    local event_data="$1"
    
    # Check if any content block is AskUserQuestion
    echo "$event_data" | jq -e \
        '.[] | select(.type == "tool_use" and .name == "AskUserQuestion")' \
        >/dev/null 2>&1
}
```

### extract_question_info()
```bash
extract_question_info() {
    local event_data="$1"
    
    # Extract the AskUserQuestion input
    local question_input
    question_input=$(echo "$event_data" | jq -c \
        '.[] | select(.name == "AskUserQuestion") | .input')
    
    # Parse first question (usually only one)
    local question header options multi_select
    question=$(echo "$question_input" | jq -r '.questions[0].question // ""')
    header=$(echo "$question_input" | jq -r '.questions[0].header // ""')
    options=$(echo "$question_input" | jq -c '.questions[0].options // []')
    multi_select=$(echo "$question_input" | jq -r '.questions[0].multiSelect // false')
    
    # Format for queue
    cat << EOF
{
  "question": $(echo "$question" | jq -R .),
  "header": $(echo "$header" | jq -R .),
  "options": $options,
  "multi_select": $multi_select,
  "tool_use_id": $(echo "$event_data" | jq '.[] | select(.name == "AskUserQuestion") | .id')
}
EOF
}
```

### detect_text_question()
Check for questions in plain text (fallback):
```bash
detect_text_question() {
    local text="$1"
    
    # Question patterns
    local -a patterns=(
        'Should I'
        'Do you want'
        'Would you like'
        'Please confirm'
        'Choose.*:'
        'Which.*\?'
        'What.*\?'
        'How should'
    )
    
    for pattern in "${patterns[@]}"; do
        if echo "$text" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
            return 0
        fi
    done
    
    return 1
}
```

### Stream Processing Loop
```bash
process_stream_json() {
    local log_file="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    
    tail -f "$log_file" | while IFS= read -r line; do
        [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
        
        local event_type event_data
        if ! parse_stream_json_event "$line" event_type event_data; then
            continue
        fi
        
        case "$event_type" in
            system)
                $callback "init" "$event_data"
                ;;
            assistant)
                if detect_ask_user_question "$event_data"; then
                    local question_info
                    question_info=$(extract_question_info "$event_data")
                    $callback "question" "$question_info"
                fi
                ;;
            result)
                $callback "complete" "$event_data"
                break
                ;;
        esac
    done
}
```

## Edge Cases
- Malformed JSON lines (skip with warning)
- Multiple tool_use in single message
- Nested questions (multiple questions array)
- Very long text content (truncate for display)
- Binary content in tool results

## Testing
- Parse each event type correctly
- Detect AskUserQuestion
- Extract question details
- Handle malformed input gracefully
- Text question detection works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All event types parsed correctly
- [ ] AskUserQuestion detected reliably
- [ ] Question info extracted with all fields
- [ ] Text question fallback works
- [ ] Malformed input handled gracefully

 {"id":"bd-9","title":"Phase 9: Repo List Management","description":"**EPIC: Repository List Loading & Management**

## Goal
Implement robust loading, parsing, and deduplication of repository lists from config files.

## Rationale
Users maintain lists of repos to sync. These lists support comments, blank lines, branch pinning (repo@branch), and custom local names (repo as custom-name). We must parse all this robustly and handle edge cases.

## List File Format
```
# Comments start with #
# Blank lines are ignored

https://github.com/owner/repo
owner/repo
owner/repo@develop
owner/repo as custom-name
```

## Deduplication
Multiple list files may reference the same repo. We dedupe by resolved local path, keeping the first occurrence. This prevents double-processing.

## Future: Tags
We're reserving syntax for tags: `owner/repo #tag=backend`. Not implementing now, but the parser should not break on this.

## Objective
Create isolated git worktrees for each repository being reviewed and prepare repo digest caches for incremental understanding. This prevents AI edits from affecting the main working tree and enables safe parallel reviews.

## Implementation Requirements

### 2.1 Worktree Creation
```bash
prepare_review_worktrees() {
    local repos=("$@")
    local base="$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees/$REVIEW_RUN_ID"
    mkdir -p "$base"

    for repo_info in "${repos[@]}"; do
        # Parse repo spec
        local url branch custom_name local_path repo_id
        resolve_repo_spec "$repo_spec" ...

        # Refuse to run on dirty trees
        ensure_clean_or_fail "$local_path"

        local wt_path="$base/${repo_id//\//_}"
        local wt_branch="ru/review/$REVIEW_RUN_ID/${repo_id//\//-}"

        # Fetch latest and create worktree
        git -C "$local_path" fetch --quiet
        git -C "$local_path" worktree add -b "$wt_branch" "$wt_path" "$base_ref"

        # Create .ru directory for artifacts
        mkdir -p "$wt_path/.ru"

        record_worktree_mapping "$repo_id" "$wt_path" "$wt_branch"
    done
}
```

### 2.2 Worktree Mapping File
Store mapping in `$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees/$RUN_ID/mapping.json`:
```json
{
  "owner/repo": {
    "worktree_path": "/path/to/worktree",
    "branch": "ru/review/20250108-1200/owner-repo",
    "base_ref": "main",
    "created_at": "2025-01-08T12:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

### 2.3 Portable Locking
Use flock for concurrent mapping updates:
```bash
update_mapping_with_lock() {
    (
        flock -x 200
        # Update mapping.json atomically
    ) 200>"$mapping_lockfile"
}
```

### 2.4 Branch Pin Support
Respect branch pins from repo spec (e.g., `owner/repo@develop`).
The worktree should check out the pinned ref as base.

### 2.5 Dirty Tree Handling
```bash
ensure_clean_or_fail() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    if ! git -C "$repo_path" diff --quiet HEAD; then
        log_error "$repo_path has uncommitted changes"
        return 1
    fi
}
```

## REPO DIGEST CACHE (Critical P1 Feature)

The repo digest cache eliminates repetitive "understand the codebase" work by persisting Claude's understanding across runs.

### Cache Structure
```
~/.local/state/ru/repo-digests/
├── owner_repo.md           # Cached digest content
├── owner_repo.meta.json    # Cache metadata
├── another_repo.md
└── another_repo.meta.json
```

### Cache Metadata Schema
```json
{
  "repo": "owner/repo",
  "last_commit": "abc123def456",
  "last_review_at": "2025-01-08T12:00:00Z",
  "digest_version": 1,
  "context_lines": 450,
  "key_files": ["src/main.py", "lib/core.py"],
  "patterns_detected": ["async", "factory", "repository"]
}
```

### Digest Preparation Flow
```bash
prepare_repo_digests() {
    local repos=("$@")
    local cache_dir="$RU_STATE_DIR/repo-digests"
    mkdir -p "$cache_dir"

    for repo_info in "${repos[@]}"; do
        local repo_id wt_path
        get_worktree_mapping "$repo_info" repo_id wt_path

        local digest_cache="$cache_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.md"
        local meta_cache="$cache_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.meta.json"

        if [[ -f "$digest_cache" ]] && [[ -f "$meta_cache" ]]; then
            # Copy cached digest to worktree
            cp "$digest_cache" "$wt_path/.ru/repo-digest.md"

            # Compute delta since last review
            local last_sha
            last_sha=$(jq -r '.last_commit' "$meta_cache")
            local current_sha
            current_sha=$(git -C "$wt_path" rev-parse HEAD)

            if [[ "$last_sha" != "$current_sha" ]]; then
                # Append changes section
                local changes
                changes=$(git -C "$wt_path" log --oneline "$last_sha..$current_sha" 2>/dev/null | head -20)
                if [[ -n "$changes" ]]; then
                    echo -e "\n## Changes Since Last Review\n\`\`\`\n$changes\n\`\`\`" >> "$wt_path/.ru/repo-digest.md"
                fi
                log_info "Loaded cached digest for $repo_id (with delta)"
            else
                log_info "Loaded cached digest for $repo_id (no changes)"
            fi
        else
            log_info "No cached digest for $repo_id - agent will create fresh"
        fi
    done
}
```

### Digest Cache Update (Post-Review)
```bash
update_digest_cache() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local wt_path="$2"

    local cache_dir="$RU_STATE_DIR/repo-digests"
    mkdir -p "$cache_dir"

    local digest_file="$wt_path/.ru/repo-digest.md"
    if [[ -f "$digest_file" ]]; then
        local current_sha
        current_sha=$(git -C "$wt_path" rev-parse HEAD)

        # Copy digest to cache
        cp "$digest_file" "$cache_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.md"

        # Write metadata
        jq -n \
            --arg repo "$repo_id" \
            --arg sha "$current_sha" \
            --arg ts "$(date -Iseconds)" \
            '{
                repo: $repo,
                last_commit: $sha,
                last_review_at: $ts,
                digest_version: 1
            }' > "$cache_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.meta.json"

        log_info "Updated digest cache for $repo_id"
    fi
}
```

## Purpose
Execute the full three-phase agent workflow for a single repository.

## Implementation

```bash
run_single_agent_workflow() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local with_release="${2:-false}"
    local start_time=$(date +%s%3N)

    # Preflight check
    if ! repo_preflight_check "$repo_path"; then
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "preflight" "skipped" 0 "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # ADDED: Create backup reference before any changes
    git -C "$repo_path" update-ref -m "agent-sweep backup before run $RUN_ID" \
        "refs/agent-sweep/pre-run-$RUN_ID" HEAD 2>/dev/null || true

    # Generate unique session name
    local session_name="ru_sweep_$(sanitize_session_name "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")")_$$"

    # Spawn session
    local spawn_result
    spawn_result=$(ntm_spawn_session "$session_name" "$repo_path")
    if ! json_get_field "$spawn_result" "success" | grep -q "true"; then
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "spawn" "failed" 0 "session spawn failed" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Phase 1: Understanding
    local phase1_prompt
    phase1_prompt=$(get_phase1_prompt "$repo_path")
    ntm_send_prompt "$session_name" "$phase1_prompt"

    if ! ntm_wait_completion "$session_name" "${PHASE1_TIMEOUT:-180}"; then
        ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "phase1" "timeout" "$PHASE1_TIMEOUT" "" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract and validate understanding
    local pane_output
    pane_output=$(ntm_get_pane_output "$session_name")
    local understanding
    understanding=$(extract_plan_json "$pane_output" "UNDERSTANDING")

    # Phase 2: Commit Planning
    local phase2_prompt
    phase2_prompt=$(get_phase2_prompt "$repo_path")
    ntm_send_prompt "$session_name" "$phase2_prompt"

    if ! ntm_wait_completion "$session_name" "${PHASE2_TIMEOUT:-300}"; then
        ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "phase2" "timeout" "$PHASE2_TIMEOUT" "" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract and validate commit plan
    pane_output=$(ntm_get_pane_output "$session_name")
    local commit_plan
    commit_plan=$(extract_plan_json "$pane_output" "COMMIT_PLAN")

    if ! validate_commit_plan "$commit_plan" "$repo_path"; then
        ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "validation" "failed" 0 "commit plan validation failed" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Execute commit plan (if apply mode)
    if [[ "${EXECUTION_MODE:-apply}" == "apply" ]]; then
        if ! execute_commit_plan "$commit_plan" "$repo_path"; then
            ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
            write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "execution" "failed" 0 "commit execution failed" "$repo_path"
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    # Phase 3: Release (optional)
    if [[ "$with_release" == true ]] && has_release_workflow "$repo_path"; then
        local phase3_prompt
        phase3_prompt=$(get_phase3_prompt "$repo_path")
        ntm_send_prompt "$session_name" "$phase3_prompt"

        if ! ntm_wait_completion "$session_name" "${PHASE3_TIMEOUT:-600}"; then
            ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
            write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "phase3" "timeout" "$PHASE3_TIMEOUT" "" "$repo_path"
            return 1
        fi

        # Extract and execute release plan
        pane_output=$(ntm_get_pane_output "$session_name")
        local release_plan
        release_plan=$(extract_plan_json "$pane_output" "RELEASE_PLAN")

        if [[ "${EXECUTION_MODE:-apply}" == "apply" ]]; then
            execute_release_plan "$release_plan" "$repo_path"
        fi
    fi

    # Cleanup
    ntm_kill_session "$session_name"

    local duration=$(($(date +%s%3N) - start_time))
    write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "agent-sweep" "success" "$duration" "" "$repo_path"
    mark_repo_completed "$repo_path"
    return 0
}
```

## Recovery Note
If something goes wrong, the backup ref can be used:
```bash
git show refs/agent-sweep/pre-run-<run-id>  # View original HEAD
git reset --hard refs/agent-sweep/pre-run-<run-id>  # Restore (use with care)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Creates backup reference before any changes
- [ ] Preflight check gates workflow entry
- [ ] All three phases execute in order
- [ ] Timeouts enforced per phase
- [ ] Plans extracted and validated
- [ ] Execution only in apply mode
- [ ] Results written for all outcomes
- [ ] Session cleaned up on completion/failure
